revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:太政官布告・太政官達]][[ko:태정관포고와 태정관달]]
{{Infobox Law | Name = Dajōkan Proclamation and Dajōkan Directive | Era = Meiji Period }} '''Dajōkan Proclamation''' (太政官布告) and '''Dajōkan Directive''' (太政官達) were forms of {{ill|laws|ja|法令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/laws|1=regulations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106947327}} issued by the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. == Overview == Both proclamations and directives were issued by the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}, the highest administrative body of the era. Initially, no strict distinction existed between them. However, in {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} ({{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=Meiji 6|1=1873}}), a differentiation was introduced: - **Dajōkan Directives** (達): Orders to government offices, concluding with “此旨相達候事” - **Dajōkan Proclamations** (布告): Public notices, concluding with “此旨布告候事” ({{ill|1873 Dajōkan Proclamation No. 254|ja|明治6th year 太政官布告第254号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873 Dajōkan Proclamation No. 254|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) Despite this distinction, some directives applied broadly to the public. == Abolition == The {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}} system was abolished with the {{ill|Cabinet_(government)|en|Cabinet_(government)|qid=Q640506|lt=Cabinet|1=Cabinet (government)}} in {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}}, and in {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=1886|1=1886}}, the {{ill|Kōbunshiki|ja|公文式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbunshiki|1=Kōbunshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392047}} (Meiji 19, Imperial Edict No. 1) formally ended the use of Dajōkan-style laws. == Post-Meiji Legal Validity == The {{ill|Meiji Constitution|en|Meiji Constitution|qid=Q122804|lt=Meiji Constitution|1=Meiji Constitution}} of {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} upheld the validity of pre-constitution laws unless they conflicted with new statutes. Under the {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=1947 Japanese Constitution|1=Constitution of Japan}}, the legality of pre-war laws depends on their consistency with the new constitution. == Examples of Dajōkan Proclamations Still Referenced == - **Calendar Proclamation** (改暦ノ布告, Meiji 5, No. 337): Introduced the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}. - **Hanging Instrument Diagram Regulations** (絞罪器械図式, Meiji 6, No. 65): Defined the design of execution devices. - **Decoration Ordinance** (勲章制定ノ件, Meiji 8, No. 54): Governed the conferment of state decorations.
== See Also == * {{ill|Meiji period law|ja|明治時代の法令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period law|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōbunshiki|ja|公文式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbunshiki|1=Kōbunshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392047}} * {{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=Japanese Constitution|1=Constitution of Japan}}
* [[Tensha Shinto Prohibition Ordinance|Decree Prohibiting Tensha]]
<nowiki/>
